exercises.title: Algorithm Comparison Matrix
exercises.description: Create a comprehensive comparison of NIST PQC signature algorithms.
tasks: Create a table comparing ML-DSA, SLH-DSA, and classical ECDSA P-256
Include: public key size, signature size, security assumption, quantum resistance
Calculate total bytes per signed message for each algorithm
Identify scenarios where each algorithm would be the best choice
Explain why SLH-DSA has such small public keys despite large signatures
exercises.expectedOutput: Detailed comparison matrix with clear recommendations for each use case
hints: SLH-DSA public keys are just hash outputs (32-64 bytes)
ML-DSA public keys contain polynomial matrices (~2KB)
Consider signature verification frequency vs signing frequency

exercises.title: Stateful vs Stateless Trade-off Analysis
exercises.description: Analyze the security implications of stateful vs stateless signature schemes.
tasks: Describe the state that must be maintained in XMSS/LMS
Explain what happens if a one-time key is reused in stateful schemes
Calculate how many signatures XMSS with h=20 can produce
Analyze backup/restore risks for stateful implementations
Design a scenario where stateful schemes are appropriate despite risks
exercises.expectedOutput: Comprehensive analysis of state management challenges and mitigations
hints: XMSS with height h can produce 2^h signatures
Stateful schemes need atomic state updates
Consider HSM implementations for stateful schemes

exercises.title: SLH-DSA Parameter Selection
exercises.description: Implement a parameter selection tool for SLH-DSA deployment scenarios.
tasks: Create a function that recommends SLH-DSA parameters based on requirements
Consider: security level, signing frequency, bandwidth constraints
Compare all 12 parameter sets (6 sizes × 2 variants)
Include both SHA-2 and SHAKE instantiations in analysis
Provide justified recommendations for 5 different deployment scenarios
exercises.starterCode:
# SLH-DSA Parameter Sets (FIPS 205)
SLH_DSA_PARAMS = {
    'SLH-DSA-SHA2-128f': {'pk': 32, 'sig': 17088, 'security': 128, 'variant': 'fast'},
    'SLH-DSA-SHA2-128s': {'pk': 32, 'sig': 7856, 'security': 128, 'variant': 'small'},
    'SLH-DSA-SHA2-192f': {'pk': 48, 'sig': 35664, 'security': 192, 'variant': 'fast'},
    'SLH-DSA-SHA2-192s': {'pk': 48, 'sig': 16224, 'security': 192, 'variant': 'small'},
    'SLH-DSA-SHA2-256f': {'pk': 64, 'sig': 49856, 'security': 256, 'variant': 'fast'},
    'SLH-DSA-SHA2-256s': {'pk': 64, 'sig': 29792, 'security': 256, 'variant': 'small'},
    'SLH-DSA-SHAKE-128f': {'pk': 32, 'sig': 17088, 'security': 128, 'variant': 'fast'},
    'SLH-DSA-SHAKE-128s': {'pk': 32, 'sig': 7856, 'security': 128, 'variant': 'small'},
    'SLH-DSA-SHAKE-192f': {'pk': 48, 'sig': 35664, 'security': 192, 'variant': 'fast'},
    'SLH-DSA-SHAKE-192s': {'pk': 48, 'sig': 16224, 'security': 192, 'variant': 'small'},
    'SLH-DSA-SHAKE-256f': {'pk': 64, 'sig': 49856, 'security': 256, 'variant': 'fast'},
    'SLH-DSA-SHAKE-256s': {'pk': 64, 'sig': 29792, 'security': 256, 'variant': 'small'},
}

def recommend_slh_dsa(
    min_security: int,
    max_signature_kb: float,
    signing_frequency: str,  # 'high', 'medium', 'low'
    prefer_sha2: bool = True
) -> str:
    """
    Recommend optimal SLH-DSA parameter set.
    
    Args:
        min_security: Minimum security level in bits (128, 192, or 256)
        max_signature_kb: Maximum acceptable signature size in KB
        signing_frequency: Expected signing frequency
        prefer_sha2: Whether to prefer SHA2 over SHAKE variants
    
    Returns:
        Recommended parameter set name or explanation if none suitable
    """
    # Your implementation here
    pass

# Test scenarios
scenarios = [
    {'name': 'Root CA', 'security': 256, 'max_sig': 50, 'freq': 'low'},
    {'name': 'Code Signing', 'security': 192, 'max_sig': 40, 'freq': 'medium'},
    {'name': 'Document Signing', 'security': 128, 'max_sig': 10, 'freq': 'medium'},
    {'name': 'Firmware Updates', 'security': 192, 'max_sig': 20, 'freq': 'low'},
    {'name': 'Email Signing', 'security': 128, 'max_sig': 20, 'freq': 'high'},
]

for s in scenarios:
    result = recommend_slh_dsa(s['security'], s['max_sig'], s['freq'])
    print(f"{s['name']}: {result}")
exercises.expectedOutput: Working parameter selection tool with justified recommendations
hints: For low frequency signing, prefer 's' variants for smaller signatures
For high frequency, prefer 'f' variants for faster signing
SHA-2 variants may be preferred for compatibility with existing infrastructure

exercises.title: Security Assumption Comparison
exercises.description: Research and compare the cryptographic assumptions underlying different PQC schemes.
tasks: Explain the hash function security properties SLH-DSA relies on
Compare with ML-DSA's MLWE/MSIS assumptions
Research known attacks on hash functions vs lattice problems
Analyze the impact of potential future cryptanalytic advances
Justify NIST's decision to standardize both lattice and hash-based signatures
exercises.expectedOutput: Research report on comparative security foundations
hints: SLH-DSA needs: second-preimage resistance, PRF security
Hash functions have ~30 years of cryptanalysis history
Lattice problems emerged in cryptography ~15 years ago
Consider the diversification argument for multiple standards
